sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Manchester City are in strong form, winning 4 of their last 5 matches, including impressive victories like 4-1 against Borussia Dortmund and 3-0 against Liverpool. Bayer 04 Leverkusen have also shown improvement, winning 3 of their last 5 matches, with a standout 6-0 thrashing of 1. FC Heidenheim. However, their performance against stronger teams like Bayern München (3-0 loss) highlights vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Manchester City dominate in possession (65.25%, 2nd in league) and passing accuracy (91.9%, 1st in league), with a solid defense conceding just 3 goals this season (6th in league). Leverkusen, on the other hand, have struggled defensively, conceding 10 goals in their league campaign (as per standings), and sit at 22nd with only 5 points. City's attacking efficiency (50% big chances converted, 5th in league) further tilts the balance in their favor.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: There are no recent head-to-head matchups provided for analysis. Thus, the preview relies heavily on current form and season statistics to gauge the likely outcome of this encounter.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Manchester City, Erling Haaland (4 goals in last 5 matches) and Jérémy Doku (2 goals, 1 assist) are in fine form and could be decisive. Leverkusen will rely on Ibrahim Maza (3 goals) and Patrik Schick (3 goals, 1 assist) to pose a threat upfront, though their impact may be limited against City's robust defense.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore's prediction of a 93% chance of a home win, combined with Manchester City's superior form, league position (4th vs 22nd), and statistical dominance, they are heavily favored to win at the Etihad Stadium. Leverkusen's recent wins show resilience, but their defensive frailties could be exposed by City's attacking prowess. Expect a match with high intensity and a likely comfortable win for the hosts.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Etihad Stadium saw Bayer 04 Leverkusen take a surprising lead over Manchester City with a well-taken goal by Alejandro Grimaldo in the 23rd minute. Manchester City, despite dominating possession and creating more entries into the final third, failed to convert their opportunities into goals. Leverkusen, on the other hand, were clinical with their limited chances, capitalizing on a defensive lapse to secure the advantage. Both teams showed resilience in duels, splitting aerial challenges evenly, though City edged out in overall physical contests. The home side will be frustrated with a missed big chance, while Leverkusen will look to protect their slender lead in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Manchester City started the match with significant attacking intent, maintaining pressure in the early stages with consistent forward momentum. Their dominance was evident as they pushed forward with purpose for much of the first half. However, Leverkusen gradually found their footing, showing a shift in momentum around the time of their goal with a notable spell of attacking play. After taking the lead, the away side gained confidence and had moments of sustained pressure. Towards the end of the half, City regained control with a strong surge in attacking intent, pushing hard to level the score before the break. The momentum ebbed and flowed, but City’s persistence in the closing minutes suggests they will come out aggressively in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Manchester City showcased their trademark style with a slight edge in ball possession and a higher number of final third entries and penalty area incursions compared to Leverkusen. Their ability to win possession in dangerous areas was notable, indicating potential for a breakthrough if they sharpen their finishing. Leverkusen, despite being outplayed in territorial stats, demonstrated efficiency in front of goal and a solid defensive structure that limited City’s clear-cut chances. The away side’s goalkeeper also played a key role in maintaining the lead, showing composure under pressure. Both teams avoided disciplinary issues, keeping their full complement of players for the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Manchester City, players like Oscar Bobb stood out in the first half with creative contributions and involvement in dangerous plays, which could be crucial for an equalizer. Nathan Aké also showed threat from set-pieces, narrowly missing a big opportunity. On Leverkusen’s side, Alejandro Grimaldo was the standout performer, not only scoring the opener but also contributing defensively. Their defensive unit, including players like Loïc Badé, was instrumental in clearing danger and will need to remain vigilant. Both teams have individuals capable of turning the game, and tactical adjustments could see fresh legs or key figures making the difference after the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given Manchester City’s relentless attacking approach and territorial dominance in the first half, they are likely to push hard for an equalizer early in the second half. Their ability to create chances, combined with playing at home, suggests they have the upper hand to turn the game around. Leverkusen will likely adopt a more defensive posture to protect their lead, looking for counter-attacking opportunities to catch City off-guard. However, City’s depth and quality in the final third make them favorites to at least draw level. The second half could see an increase in intensity, with the home side expected to capitalize on their chances if they maintain their pressure.
